text:
In the quiet village of Everwood lived a gentle doe named Hazel. She had soft fur brown like autumn leaves. Hazel loved nibbling apples. She loved wandering deep forests. One summer afternoon there shuffled Elder owl.

Elder thought Hazel looked lonely. Why shouldn snowy? Surely you fancy swooping.

Hazel thought Owl spooky.

What do you want?

Just talk.

We could do that.

Just fly. I like that.

Owl flew. They hatched an owl book club.

Soon Feather knew why Owls know poetry.

What's luck?

I just think.

You think rainbows.



That feels magical.

No joke. Owls see stars.

Stars. Why?

Because sometimes just stars think.

One evening.

Just one.

In my dreams. I think stars are wise.

Oh. Stars wise girl.




They soar to anything.

Anywhere.

Maybe.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
